components.description: Deal additional Weapon Damage when in close range to your target.

sections.Sources of Melee Damage: Apart from light and heavy melee attacks, some hero abilities are also considered to deal melee damage. Generally if an ability scales with melee damage than its damage is reduced by Melee Resist.

sections.Trivia: This item was previously named Basic Magazine . In certain locations around the Map , Extended Magazine has its own promotional poster.

components.description: Your next headshot against an enemy Hero deals bonus weapon damage .

sections.Notes: The bonus headshot damage does not apply to enemy Troopers , or any other NPC in possession of a headshot hitbox. The bonus headshot damage is affected by your Hero's Damage Falloff variables.

sections.Trivia: This item was previously named High-Velocity Mag . This item was inspired by a forum post by user @DNADota . [ 1 ]

sections.Notes: Monster Rounds applies additional damage to Non-Player Characters. This includes Troopers , lane structures like Guardians , Walkers , neutral entities such as Denizen , and the Patron . It does apply additional damage against Graves ' Ghouls , but not against McGinnis ' Mini Turrets .

components.description: Your next bullet will heal you based on what target you hit.

sections.Notes: Restorative Shot goes on cooldown regardless if the bullet hits a target.

components.description: While reloading, pressing Reload during the highlighted portion will instantly finish your reload and grant you Fire Rate , Bullet Lifesteal and Move Speed .

sections.Notes: If the player presses the reload button while outside the highlighted section, the item will fail to reload. The item only goes into cooldown once the effect is activated. The duration of the Active Reload activation window is always 0.3s. The highlighted section changes size proportionately to the hero's Reload Time .
sections.Notable Interactions: Vyper benefits from this item to have the smallest downtime to use her ammo sliding synergy. McGinnis can use this item to offset her long reload time.

components.description: Gain bonus Move Speed and Slow Resistance .

sections.Trivia: Fleetfoot's icon is similar to the demon Buer , from the grimoire Pseudomonarchia Daemonum , and is also similar to the Sicily Trinacria .

components.description: Increases Weapon Damage as you continuously fire your weapon.

sections.Mechanics: Intensifying Magazine ramps up the bonus weapon damage from 0% to 45% while the player holds down the Fire button and their weapon is in a firing state. Some abilities will still flag the hero as "firing" while the character isn't actually firing, but if the user continues to hold down their Fire button then they will continue charging up their bonus weapon damage. This can be used to charge up the bonus damage before a fight or maintain the bonus damage for longer.
sections.Abilities that continue to allow buildin: Bebop 's Exploding Uppercut Vindicta 's Assassinate (while scoped in) McGinnis ' Spectral Wall (Must be holding Fire before casting the wall) Pressing the Alt-Fire button will not increase the bonus weapon damage, but players can hold both Fire and Alt-Fire at the same time. This will cause heroes to continuously use their Alternate fire but still build bonus damage.

components.description: When you Dash-Jump you gain Fire Rate and bonus Ammo until your next reload. Lasts up to 7s.
Deal additional Weapon Damage when beyond a minimum distance from your target.

sections.Trivia: Long Range's icon is a reference to the movie A Trip to the Moon by Georges Méliès.

components.description: Your next Heavy Melee attack against an enemy deals increased damage .

components.description: Your next bullet deals bonus spirit damage .

sections.Notes: The item provides a passive effect that creates an additional bullet when the hero shoots their weapon, and the additional bullet will mimic the bullets created by the current weapon. The Mystic Shot bullet appears as a glowing purple version of the hero's regular bullets. For most weapons, the Mystic bullet can follow the trajectory of any bullet, which can make it fly off-center for weapons with significant spread over time, such as McGinnis '. The exception are spreadshot weapons such as Abrams ' and Shiv 's, in which the Mystic pellet is always fired at the center of the crosshair. For explosive alternate fire weapons such as Viscous ' and Yamato 's, the Mystic bullet will appear the same but cannot deal damage in an area. Instead, it only deals the Spirit damage to the closest enemy to impact. The Mystic bullet hits before regular bullets. So for example, Bullet Resist Shredder 's effect is applied first and amplifies the weapon damage, but Crippling Headshot 's Spirit Resist reduction is applied after and does not amplify Mystic Shot's spirit damage. The additional Mystic bullet cannot deal critical (headshot) damage and is not affect by Ricochet . A Mystic bullet is immune to evasion .

components.description: Your attacks have additional Weapon Damage against enemies above 50% health .

sections.Trivia: This item was previously named Pristine Emblem .

components.description: Dealing significant weapon damage replenishes a charge for each of your charged abilities .

components.description: Your bullets build up a Movement Slow on enemies.

components.description: Your bullets apply a debuff that reduces the Spirit Resist of the target and grants you and your allies Spirit Lifesteal against them.

sections.Notes: The Spirit Lifesteal from the debuff is independent of the diminishing returns from other sources of spirit lifesteal. It will always grant the player a flat +10% spirit lifesteal. The debuff can be applied with melee attacks, as well as abilities that are considered melee attacks.
sections.Trivia: This item was previously named Soul Shredder Bullets .

components.description: Make your weapon fire multishot . Hitting more than one Hero per attack will grant a stacking weapon damage bonus . Targets can only be hit once per multishot.

sections.Notes: Multishots can hit units as well, but they do not grant stacks. Each shot is separated by approximately 5°, leading to a total of approximately 20° spread between the furthest two shots.
sections.Notable interactions: For Celeste , it splits into 3 shots at 70° spread. Split Shot affects Grey Talon 's Rain of Arrows , allowing him to fire 25 arrows at once. Split Shot affects Paradox 's Kinetic Carbine , allowing her to fire 5 Kinetic Carbine shots at once. However, it will only affect the first enemy hit. Paige and The Doorman can gain stacks by hitting multiple players with the same shot. Split Shot has no effect on Silver (Transformed) .

components.description: Dealing weapon damage at close range opens a wound and grants you bonus move speed . Wounded enemies take spirit damage over time , have reduced bullet resist , and are revealed through walls .

sections.Notes: Stalker's damage triggers any "on spirit damage" effects like Mystic Slow or Bullet Resist Shredder Stalker procs off any ability that deals physical damage if the player was close enough. Your spirit damage applies Healing Reduction . If an enemy hero dies under this effect, you receive a large heal.

components.description: Increases the effectiveness of your healing .

sections.Notes: Healing booster affects Bullet Lifesteal and Spirit Lifesteal Healing booster affects Health Regen only if it's a temporary effect (e.g. Frozen Shelter ). It does not affect constant regen acquired from most items and rare abilities. Healing Booster increases the effect of healing applied to allies as well as the caster, but owning Healing Booster does not increase the healing received from an ally.
sections.Amplified sources of healing: Following abilities and items get their healings amplified by Healing Booster.

components.description: When an enemy uses an ability within 35m range from you, store one Restoration Stack . Consume all stacks to heal yourself and replenish up to 3 stamina based on how many stacks you have.

sections.Notes: Non-Active Abilities and Items do not trigger this passive effect.

components.description: Automatically fire a bullet towards any attacker who damages you with their abilities or weapon.

sections.Notes: Damage Reflection Mechanics Returns the attacker's pre-mitigation damage (original damage before your resistances apply) Example: If an enemy deals 100 Bullet Damage while you have 30% Bullet Resist , you reflect 65 damage, and you take 70. Works even with 100% damage immunity (e.g., Metal Skin reflects full damage while preventing damage to the user). Does not apply build-up or stacking effects against the attacker.

components.description: Gain a Barrier whenever you take significant spirit damage from enemy Heroes in a small time frame.
Gain a Barrier whenever you take significant weapon damage from enemy Heroes in a small time frame.

passives: +7 Bonus Spirit Power for Charged Abilities

sections.Notable interactions: If Sinclair copies Vindicta 's Assassinate with Extra Charge, they will start with 2 charges. After using Assassinate once, they can keep the copy indefinitely as long as they have one or more charges left.

components.description: Hatch the egg, gaining souls and permanent buffs . The value of the egg grows the longer you hold onto it. Gain a permanent buff per 80 Souls accrued when hatched.

sections.Notes: The soul value starts at 400 . These initial souls do not count towards the buffs gained. It takes 4m 27s for its value to match its cost ( 800 ). The buffs received follow the scaling of Golden Statue buffs at the time the egg is hatched. The buffs increase in value after 10 minutes of match duration, and then again after 30 minutes. It does not provide buffs in testing modes.
sections.Trivia: Golden Goose Egg is the first consumable item added to Deadlock . The item's name is a reference to the Aesop's Fable The Goose that Laid the Golden Eggs .

components.description: Charges up over time with bonus spirit damage , causing abilities dealing more than 80 damage to deal additional damage.

sections.Notes: Mystic Burst uses Charge-Up Heroes with Spirit Lifesteal will heal from Mystic Burst's passive effect triggering. A small lightning shock visual is applied to the enemy hero when it is hit by Mystic Burst's passive effect. When the target is hit with spirit damage , the damage value which is compared against the triggering threshold is the base damage value prior to any increases or decreases from damage amp, damage reduction, spirit resistance, and spirit resistance reduction. Damage amp and spirit resist reduction will not help you in achieving the damage threshold to trigger the passive. Damage reduction and the target's spirit resistance will not prevent the passive from triggering when it otherwise would. While the tooltip only specifies 'Abilities', only Spirit Damage will activate Mystic Burst. Abilities dealing weapon damage - such as Venator 's Consecrating Grenade or Viscous ' Puddle Punch - will not activate Mystic Burst. The additional damage, and the ability which triggered the additional damage, are applied to the target as separate instances of damage. The additional damage from Mystic Burst is dealt after the original spell damage.

components.description: Imbue an ability to increase its range and effect radius .

sections.Trivia and Gallery: This item was previously named Mystic Reach . Mystic Expansion was one of several items to feature in promotional posters around the Map . Poster for Mystic Reach Comparing the Slice and Dice ability distance of a Shiv with Mystic Expansion and one without.

components.description: Dealing spirit damage to enemy Heroes grants you Bonus regeneration . Stacks when dealing damage to different heroes.
Target an enemy to reduce their Fire Rate and Bullet Resistance .

sections.Trivia: It was previously named Withering Whip .

components.description: When you perform a Light or Heavy Melee attack against a hero, deal extra spirit damage with the attack and reduce the target's Spirit Resist . Cooldown is 2x longer for Light Melee hits.
After you Dash-Jump , the next ability you use within ArcaneSurgeWindows will have bonus Range, Duration, and Spirit Power .

sections.Notes: Items and passive abilities cannot benefit from the conditional buff - they will not gain additional spirit power, duration, or range. The buff which provides the player with the conditional ability range, ability duration, and spirit power lasts for 7 seconds. The first ability cast after gaining the buff will consume the buff. The conditional ability range and ability duration are snapshot on ability use. The conditional spirit power is tied to the 7 second timer of the conditional buff. The +20 spirit power will only be available for 7 seconds after the player initially gains the conditional buff. Examples: 6 seconds after gaining the conditional buff, Vindicta uses Assassinate . This consumes the buff, and applies +20 spirit power to Assassinate 's damage. 6 seconds after gaining the conditional buff, Seven uses Lightning Ball . The buff is consumed. The Lightning Ball gains +15% ability duration and +12% ability range. The Lightning Ball gains +20 spirit power for 1 second only. For the rest of its duration, it does not benefit from +20 spirit power. 6 seconds after gaining the conditional buff, Seven uses Power Surge . The buff is consumed. Power Surge gains +15% ability duration and +12% ability range. Power Surge 's damage benefits from +20 spirit power for 1 second only. For the rest of its duration, the damage does not benefit from +20 spirit power. Power Surge 's total duration scales natively with spirit power at time of cast. The additional duration due to the +20 spirit power is snapshot on cast, and is not lost when the 7s conditional buff is consumed.

components.description: Reduces Bullet Resist on enemies when you deal spirit damage .

sections.Notes: This is not a stacking effect, repeated applications will refresh the duration of the debuff. The debuff can be utilized by other players. Teammates can benefit from this debuff.

components.description: Release an expanding ice blast that deals spirit damage and Slows targets it hits.

sections.Notes: The wave expands out from the hero, reaching the maximum radius after about a second. The damage and effect is applied to enemies when the wave hits them, making it possible to be avoided if the hero is fast enough.

components.description: Imbue an ability to reduce its Cooldown .

sections.Notes: If Compress Cooldown is imbued on Sinclair 's Audience Participation , it does not reduce the cooldown of copied ultimates, only the default cooldown of the ability.
sections.Trivia and Gallery: This item was previously named Improved Cooldown. Compress Cooldown was one of several items to feature in promotional posters around the Map . Poster for Improved Cooldown Seven 's Storm Cloud ability cooldown after casting, one without Cooldown Reduction (top), and one with Compress Cooldown (bottom).

components.description: Imbue an ability to increase its Duration .
When the target takes spirit damage , they have their Move Speed reduced.
When an enemy takes spirit damage , they have their spirit resist reduced.

sections.Notes: Unlike Escalating Exposure , Mystic Vulnerability does not cause a stacking effect. Hitting the enemy with additional spirit damage after the debuff has been applied will only refresh the duration. Escalating Exposure's debuff takes priority over the Mystic Vulnerability debuff, so if two players have each of these items they will not stack together on the same target.

components.description: Your imbued ability charges up over time with bonus spirit damage , bonus fire rate , and reloads bullets on use.

sections.Notes: Quicksilver Reload uses Charge-Up . Quicksilver Reload's bonus Spirit Damage will only trigger when the imbued ability first deals Weapon Damage or Spirit Damage to the target. If the initial damage is reduced to 0 (rounded down) or only deals Pure Damage (i.e. Lady Geist 's Soul Exchange ), Quicksilver Reload's bonus Spirit Damage is not applied. Abilities that do not deal damage still trigger the instant reload and bonus Fire Rate effects. The passive buff has a fixed duration of 12 seconds and is not affected by cooldown reduction.
sections.Notable Interactions: Heroes with long reload times and quick-charging or multi-charge abilities can make good usage of Quicksilver: McGinnis with multiple turret charges Infernus with multiple Napalm charges Haze who benefits from maintaining Fixation stacks on targeted enemies Vindicta Crow Familiar has a short cooldown after second upgrade Mo & Krill Scorn has a short cooldown and heals a percent of damage dealt

components.description: Slows movement of enemy target. Also Silences their movement-based items and abilities . Increases the target's gravity. Does not affect target's stamina usage.

sections.Notes: Slowing Hex sets a player's gravity to 120% (makes them heavier), regardless of their base gravity. This especially affects Celeste and Ivy , who have innate gravity reduction. Slowing Hex does not disable passive effects such as Vyper 's Slither (though it appears visually as though it was disabled). The Doorman 's Doorway and Mo & Krill 's Burrow are not affected by Slowing Hex.
sections.Hero abilities silenced by Slowing Hex: Abrams - Shoulder Charge and Seismic Impact Apollo - Disengaging Sigil , Flawless Advance , and Itani Lo Sahn Billy - Rising Ram Calico - Leaping Slash , Ava , and Return to Shadows Drifter - Stalker's Mark 's second activation Ambush (activating the ability isn't silenced) Dynamo - Quantum Entanglement Grey Talon - Rain of Arrows Holliday - Bounce Pad Infernus - Flame Dash Ivy - Air Drop Kelvin - Ice Path Lash - Grapple Mina - Sanguine Retreat Mirage - Dust Devil Pocket - Flying Cloak 's second activation Teleport (activating the ability isn't silenced) Rem - Tag Along Shiv - Slice and Dice and Killing Blow Silver - Boot Kick and Mauling Leap Sinclair - Spectral Assistant 's second activation Teleport (activating the ability isn't silenced) Vindicta - Flight Wraith - Project Mind Yamato - Flying Slash
sections.Items silenced by Slowing Hex: Imbue Warp Stone Active Imbue Majestic Leap Active Imbue Phantom Strike Active Imbue Magic Carpet Active

components.description: Target an enemy to reduce their Spirit Resist and Spirit Power .
When you deal spirit damage to enemies, you also reduce their Fire Rate .
Gain a Barrier for a short duration.
